mechanism: Counter-cyclical expansion of SNAP and farm bill spending during consolidation attempts creates fiscal convexity that automatically increases spending in politically protected sectors, narrowing the feasible consolidation corridor. Farm-state senators (40+ seats) can veto packages that cut these programs.
regime_dependence: Structurally embedded in US fiscal architecture; activates specifically during consolidation attempts that reduce growth or lower crop prices. The trap is stronger during periods of agricultural commodity price weakness.
